response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of software experience but lacks the critical requirements for this role. There is no mention of Japanese language proficiency (a hard requirement for native-level fluency), no demonstrated Linux kernel modification experience, no embedded firmware or LAN-switch background, and no exposure to the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). C programming experience is limited to a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments a decade ago; the candidate's core stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web/cloud work.
strengths: Long, diverse software engineering career with senior/staff-level roles
Comfortable working across multiple languages and stacks (C/C++, DLang, JS/TS, Java)
concerns: No evidence of Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement)
No Linux kernel source modification or embedded firmware experience
No exposure to network-security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X) or L2/L3 switch development

key_factors.factor: C for UNIX experience is dated and minimal
